perturbation.original_text: DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I, _______________________________________, an individual official, employee, consultant, or subcontractor of or to _____________________ (the Authorized Entity), intending to be legally bound, hereby consent to the terms in this Agreement in consideration of my being granted conditional access to certain information, specified below, that is owned by, produced by, or in the possession of the United States Government.
perturbation.changed_text: DEPARTMENT OF HOMELAND SECURITY NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT I, _______________________________________, an individual official, employee, contractor, or vendor of or to _____________________ (the Authorized Entity), intending to be legally bound, hereby consent to the terms in this Agreement in consideration of my being granted conditional access to certain information, specified below, that is owned by, produced by, or in the possession of the United States Government.
perturbation.explanation: The change of 'consultant, or subcontractor' to 'contractor, or vendor' introduces ambiguity. While the original text lists specific roles, the modified text uses broader terms. The term contractor encompasses consultants and subcontractors and vendor is usually for parties that provide physical goods. It's uncertain now whether all consultants and subcontractors are considered 'contractors' or 'vendors' for the purposes of this agreement, especially concerning liability and responsibilities. This creates contradiction in section 5(a), where employee, consultant, or subcontractor are listed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession. (2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, I will not request, obtain, maintain, or use PCII unless the PCII Program Manager or Program Manager’s designee has first made in writing, with respect to the contractor, the certification as provided for in Section 29.8(c) of the implementing regulations to the CII Act, as amended. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in my possession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or support to DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses specific roles: 'consultant, or subcontractor.' The changed text broadens this to 'contractor, or vendor.' The explanation states this introduces ambiguity because it's uncertain whether all consultants and subcontractors are considered 'contractors' or 'vendors' for the purposes of the agreement. This directly contradicts section 5(a) which explicitly lists 'employee, consultant, or subcontractor,' creating a strong in-text contradiction because the altered definition directly affects the individuals covered by the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU) As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified information is an over-arching term that covers any information, not otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled under Section 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy.
perturbation.changed_text: Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU) As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified data is an over-arching term that covers any information, not otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled under Section 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy.
perturbation.explanation: The term "information" is replaced by "data" creating an ambiguous case where the distinction of information and data is not clear and creates conflicting scenarios. In section 6 the term information is used and can create an in-text contradiction now.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. I hereby agree that I will not alter or remove markings, which indicate a category of information or require specific handling instructions, from any material I may come in contact with, in the case of SSI or SBU, unless such alteration or removal is consistent with the requirements set forth in the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific category of information or, in the case of PCII, unless such alteration or removal is authorized by the PCII Program Manager or the PCII Program Manager’s designee. I agree that if I use information from a sensitive document or other medium, I will carry forward any markings or other required restrictions to derivative products, and will protect them in the same matter as the original.
perturbation.justification: The change from "information" to "data" in the definition of Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU) creates an ambiguity. Section 6 refers to a "category of information", and if "information" and "data" are no longer considered interchangeable due to the change in definition, it's unclear whether the obligations in Section 6 apply to "SBU data". This creates a direct conflict and uncertainty about whether data is covered by the requirement not to alter or remove markings, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: I understand that the United States Government may conduct inspections, at any time or place, for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the conditions for access, dissemination, handling and safeguarding information under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: I understand that the Department of Homeland Security may conduct audits, at any time or place, for the purpose of ensuring compliance with the conditions for access, dissemination, handling and safeguarding information under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'United States Government' with 'Department of Homeland Security' narrows the scope of entities that can conduct inspections, but the agreement is with the US government, so this causes the agreement to be broken in cases where other entities of the United States Government, not part of DHS, wants to conduct audits. This leads to an in-text contradiction since section 10 states the agreement is also for the benefit of the US government.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. This Agreement is made and intended for the benefit of the United States Government and may be enforced by the United States Government or the Authorized Entity. By granting me conditional access to information in this context, the United States Government and, with respect to PCII, the Authorized Entity, may seek any remedy available to it to enforce this Agreement including, but not limited to, application for a court order prohibiting disclosure of information in breach of this Agreement. I understand that if I violate the terms and conditions of this Agreement, I could be subjected to administrative, disciplinary, civil, or criminal action, as appropriate, under the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the category of information involved and neither the United States Government nor the Authorized Entity have waived any statutory or common law evidentiary privileges or protections that they may assert in any administrative or court proceeding to protect any sensitive information to which I have been given conditional access under the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'United States Government' to 'Department of Homeland Security' narrows the scope of auditing entities. The contradicted text, section 10, states that the agreement benefits and is enforced by the entire 'United States Government'. By limiting inspections to DHS, the changed text contradicts section 10, because it would prevent other entities within the US government from conducting inspections to ensure compliance, creating an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because changing the term creates a conflict.

perturbation.original_text: I will honor and comply with any and all dissemination restrictions cited or verbally relayed to me by the proper authority.
perturbation.changed_text: I will honor and comply with any and all dissemination restrictions cited or verbally relayed to me by my supervisor.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'proper authority' to 'my supervisor' limits the scope of who can impose restrictions. This conflicts with the broader responsibilities outlined elsewhere in the agreement, as 'proper authority' can include other officials or entities besides just the supervisor. In this case, the conflict comes from section 5, where the agreement is with 'an authorized individual'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession. (2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, I will not request, obtain, maintain, or use PCII unless the PCII Program Manager or Program Manager’s designee has first made in writing, with respect to the contractor, the certification as provided for in Section 29.8(c) of the implementing regulations to the CII Act, as amended. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in my possession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or support to DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the individual will comply with dissemination restrictions cited by 'proper authority'. The changed text narrows this to 'my supervisor'. The contradicted text refers to 'an authorized individual'. By limiting the scope of who can impose restrictions to the supervisor, the changed text contradicts section 5 which refers to 'authorized individual' which is a broader term than just the supervisor. This creates a direct conflict, as an authorized individual could give a dissemination order and it would have to be followed in the original, but not in the changed text.

perturbation.original_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to the appropriate official, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved.
perturbation.changed_text: I hereby agree that I shall promptly report to my direct supervisor, in accordance with the guidance issued for the applicable category of information, any loss, theft, misuse, misplacement, unauthorized disclosure, or other security violation, I have knowledge of and whether or not I am personally involved.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'the appropriate official' to 'my direct supervisor' limits who the report should go to, and now creates an in-text contradiction since there are more authorities to report to, like the PCII Program Manager. The conflict comes from section 5(a), where it states that information is returned to a 'PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. (a) For PCII - (1) Upon the completion of my engagement as an employee, consultant, or subcontractor under the contract, or the completion of my work on the PCII Program, whichever occurs first, I will surrender promptly to the PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer, PCII of any type whatsoever that is in my possession. (2) If the Authorized Entity is a United States Government contractor performing services in support of the PCII Program, I will not request, obtain, maintain, or use PCII unless the PCII Program Manager or Program Manager’s designee has first made in writing, with respect to the contractor, the certification as provided for in Section 29.8(c) of the implementing regulations to the CII Act, as amended. (b) For SSI and SBU - I hereby agree that material which I have in my possession and containing information covered by this Agreement, will be handled and safeguarded in a manner that affords sufficient protection to prevent the unauthorized disclosure of or inadvertent access to such information, consistent with the laws, regulations, or directives applicable to the specific categories of information. I agree that I shall return all information to which I have had access or which is in my possession 1) upon demand by an authorized individual; and/or 2) upon the conclusion of my duties, association, or support to DHS; and/or 3) upon the determination that my official duties do not require further access to such information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'the appropriate official' to 'my direct supervisor', which narrows the reporting obligation. This directly contradicts section 5(a) of the contradicted text, which states that PCII should be surrendered to the 'PCII Program Manager or his designee, or to the appropriate PCII officer'. The change creates a strong in-text contradiction because the employee is now only instructed to report to their supervisor, and not to the other entities specified in section 5(a), creating uncertainty and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: This may serve as a basis for denying me conditional access to other types of information, to include classified national security information.
perturbation.changed_text: This may serve as a basis for denying me access to other types of data, to include classified national security information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'data' makes it unclear whether the denial of access applies to all types of information, or just 'data' as redefined earlier. This creates an in-text contradiction with the previous definition in the document, where the 'sensitive but unclassified data is an over-arching term that covers any information'.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Top of the document, in the definition of SBU
perturbation.contradicted_text: Other Sensitive but Unclassified (SBU) As used in this Agreement, sensitive but unclassified data is an over-arching term that covers any information, not otherwise indicated above, which the loss of, misuse of, or unauthorized access to or modification of could adversely affect the national interest or the conduct of Federal programs, or the privacy to which individuals are entitled under Section 552a of Title 5, as amended, but which has not been specifically authorized under criteria established by an Executive Order or an Act of Congress to be kept secret in the interest of national defense or foreign policy.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'data' creates a contradiction because the contradicted text defines 'sensitive but unclassified data' as an overarching term that covers 'any information.' By narrowing the scope of the denial of access to 'data' instead of 'information,' the modified text implies that access to other types of information (that are not considered 'data' under the new, narrower definition) might not be denied, directly conflicting with the original definition that encompasses all information. The explanation also highlights that the sensitive but unclassified data is an over-arching term that covers any information, leading to a contradiction.
